title: Data Table
description: A sortable, selectable table for displaying tabular data with headers, checkboxes, and action columns.
whenToUse: For displaying structured tabular data. When users need to sort, filter, or select rows. For data review and bulk actions.
whenNotToUse: For simple key/value pairs — use a definition list. For highly complex data — consider a specialized grid component outside UDS.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders semantic <table>, <thead>, <tbody>, with scope="col" on headers
Sortable headers expose aria-sort and cycle none → asc → desc on activation
Selectable mode adds a leading checkbox column and a header "select all" checkbox using aria-checked="mixed" for partial selection
Row click fires row-click only when the click did not originate from a checkbox or action cell
Striped rows, default density, and compact density all render correctly
Empty state, error state, and loading state are visually distinct and reachable via the documented props/slots
Action cells render UDS buttons with consistent sizing across densities
Badge and action cell types compose with the existing Badge and Button components without re-implementing styling
At ≥ 200 rows, performance notes recommend virtualization (see Performance section)

props.name: columns
props.description: Array of { id, header, sortable?, align?, type? } where type ∈ text|badge|action|number

props.name: rows
props.description: Array of row objects keyed by column id

props.name: selectable
props.description: Adds a checkbox column for row selection

props.name: selectedIds
props.description: Array of currently selected row ids

props.name: sortable
props.description: Allows clicking column headers to sort. Per-column override via column.sortable.

props.name: data-density
props.description: Compact density tightens padding for high-volume tables

props.name: stripedRows
props.description: Alternates row backgrounds for scannability in long tables

props.name: emptyState
props.description: Copy or component to render when rows is empty

props.name: errorState
props.description: Copy or component to render when load failed

events.name: sort
events.description: Fires when a sortable header is activated

events.name: select
events.description: Fires when a row's checkbox is toggled

events.name: select-all
events.description: Fires when the header checkbox is toggled

events.name: row-click
events.description: Fires when a row body is clicked (not the checkbox)

events.name: action
events.description: Fires when an action-cell button is clicked

slots.name: header
slots.description: Optional toolbar above the table for filters, search, bulk actions

slots.name: footer
slots.description: Optional footer for pagination or summary rows

slots.name: empty
slots.description: Custom empty-state content; replaces emptyState prop when present

states.name: loading
states.description: Skeleton or spinner; columns visible, rows replaced by placeholders

states.name: empty
states.description: No matching rows; emptyState slot/prop is rendered

states.name: error
states.description: Load failed; errorState slot/prop is rendered

states.name: row-hover
states.description: Pointer over a row tints its background

states.name: row-selected
states.description: Row checkbox checked — data-selected="true" on the <tr>

states.name: header-sorted
states.description: Active sort column shows direction chevron

contentGuidelines: Column headers: noun phrase, sentence case. Numeric columns right-aligned with monospace tabular numerals. Empty state: explain why and offer the next action ("No tenants yet — Add tenant"). Error state: describe what failed and how to retry.

dos: Use semantic table markup; the assistive-tech model depends on <table> structure
Right-align numeric columns and use tabular numerals
Preserve sort and selection across pagination — don't silently drop them
Pair selection with a bulk-action toolbar above the table when count > 0
donts: Don't use a data table for layout — that's what grid is for
Don't make the entire row clickable AND have an action button in the same row without explicit role distinction
Don't hide the column count from the empty state — explain why nothing is shown

keyboard.action: Move focus through interactive elements (sort buttons, checkboxes, action buttons)
Cycle sort direction (none → asc → desc → none)
Move focus between cells when grid mode is enabled (out of scope for 0.2)

screenReader.trigger: Sort header receives focus and is sorted
screenReader.announcement: Column header [name], sortable, currently sorted [ascending|descending] — announced via aria-sort
Row [N of M], [row label], checkbox, [checked|not checked]
All rows selected (or deselected) — uses aria-checked="mixed" for partial selection

wcag.howMet: Reading order is row-major and matches visible order
All interactive cells (sort, checkbox, action) are reachable via Tab
Visible focus ring on every interactive cell
Header text, body text, and badges all meet 4.5:1 in light/dark modes
Row borders and selected-row highlight meet 3:1 against adjacent surfaces

knownIssues: Keyboard grid navigation (cell-by-cell with arrow keys) is not yet specified — tab through interactive cells is the only supported model in 0.2
performance: Virtualize rows at ≥ 200 visible rows. Avoid rendering all rows when total > 1,000. Sort and filter should run synchronously up to ~5,000 rows; defer to a worker beyond that.
visualHierarchy: The table is the primary content of its surface. Filters, search, and bulk-action buttons sit in a toolbar above; pagination or load-more sits below.
densityBehavior: Default density matches form spacing (comfortable). Compact density reduces row height by ~25% for high-volume admin tables. Don't switch density per-row — it's a table-level decision.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Use semantic <table>, <thead>, <tbody>. Sortable headers announce current sort direction via aria-sort.
